findings.evidence: SquareClassGroup.lean:46-53 defines `squareClass` and `@[simp] squareClass_eq_zero_iff`; ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean exposes only the type, instances, and two cardinality lemmas — `card_elementaryTwoQuotient` itself reaches into `Subgroup.square`/`MonoidHom.mem_ker` rather than a reusable membership lemma.
findings.fix: Add the class map (e.g. `elementaryTwoQuotientMk : ClassGroup R → elementaryTwoQuotient R := QuotientAddGroup.mk ∘ Additive.ofMul`) and its characteristic `@[simp]` lemma `... = 0 ↔ IsSquare C`, mirroring `squareClass`/`squareClass_eq_zero_iff`.
findings.issue: `elementaryTwoQuotient` has no introduction map and no zero-characterization lemma. The parallel `SquareClassGroup.lean` provides `squareClass` plus `@[simp] squareClass_eq_zero_iff` (class is 0 ↔ IsSquare); the elementary-2 quotient — the object Layer 3's `Gal(K_gen/K) ≅ Cl(K)/Cl(K)²` and Layer 2's ambiguous-class-number formula route through — has neither, so a consumer must unfold the abbrev to `QuotientAddGroup.mk`/`eq_zero_iff` plus `Subgroup.mem_square` to relate a class to its image.

summary: Minimal, well-annotated surface with no over-exposure, but the new quotient ships without its characteristic introduction/zero lemma: there is no way to map a class into `elementaryTwoQuotient` and state "its class is trivial iff it is a square" without unfolding the abbrev to raw `QuotientAddGroup`.
